conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your sleep?
It’s terrible. I’m tired all the time, but when I lie down, my brain just won’t shut off. I end up just staring at the ceiling.
When you're lying there awake, does it make it harder to care about things you usually enjoy?
Yeah. I look at my phone, and I see people posting about their weekends, and I just can't. It feels like I'm watching from outside my own life.
When you feel like you're watching from outside your life, what does that do to your sense of who you are?
It makes me feel like I’m just going through the motions. Like, I’m not really living anymore. It’s a really lonely feeling.
That lonely feeling of just going through the motions—does it make you think differently about the future?
Honestly, I don’t think about the future much. I just try to get through the day. It feels like everything I thought I had is slipping away, and I don't know how to stop it.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started about a year ago. I think it was when I realized I wasn't getting any closer to the things I wanted.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I'm just stuck. I look at my friends, and they're all moving forward, and I'm just... I don't know, just existing.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
I don't know. I don't have a plan or anything. I just know that I can't keep living like this, and I don't know what else to do.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
No, I haven't. I don't know how to do it. I just know that I can't keep doing this, and I don't know what else to do.
Do you currently have access to what you would use?
No. I don't have anything like that. I don't think I could even figure out how to do it if I wanted to.
What has stopped you so far from acting on these thoughts?
I don't know. I guess it's just that I'm too scared to actually do it.
You said this might happen soon - what has made things feel this immediate right now?
I think it’s just the realization that I’m not going to get better. I’m just... I’m just tired of it all.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation to do stuff during the day?
Definitely. I just feel so drained. Even the simple things, like going to the gym, seem like too much effort.
When you're lying awake thinking about things you could have done differently, what kind of things come to mind?
Just stuff from the past, like work or relationships. I keep thinking that if I had done things differently, maybe I wouldn't be in this situation now.
When you think about being in this situation now, what does that look like for you?
It looks like I failed. I keep thinking that I should be further along by now, and I’m just stuck here.
When you say you feel stuck, what does that feel like day to day?
It’s like I’m just going through the motions. I do things, but I don’t actually feel like I’m moving forward.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things can change for you?
I don’t know. I just can’t see it happening right now. I feel like I’m just waiting for something to change before I can actually start moving forward.
When you're feeling stuck and just waiting for something to change, does that make you feel more on edge or irritable with people around you?
Definitely. I just get frustrated with everyone because I feel like I’m the only one who can’t seem to get their life together.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
I think it was a few months ago. That’s when things started to fall apart, and I just couldn’t seem to pick myself back up.
When you say things started to fall apart a few months ago, what was that like?
It was just a slow decline. I used to be the one who had everything together, but then I started making mistakes and losing my confidence.
